item.name: E2E — Country CRUD lifecycle

request.description: Creates PT — Portugal. Extracts code and Location header for subsequent steps.

exec: // Idempotent setup: remove PT if left over from a previous run

request.description: Reads the country created in step 1.
Updates the country name. Captures the new name for the next assertion step.
Confirms the update persisted.
Deletes the country. Expects 204 No Content.
Confirms the resource no longer exists after deletion.

item.description: Full create → read → update → verify → delete → verify-gone chain for the Country resource. Run steps in order; countryCode is propagated automatically via environment variables.

item.name: E2E — Country search flow

request.description: Inserts a unique country so search results are deterministic.
Paginated list; verifies the response is an array with at least one entry.
Full-text search; expects at least one match for the country created in step 1.
Removes the test country created in step 1 to leave the database clean.

item.description: Creates a country, verifies it appears in list and search results, then cleans up. Demonstrates chained reads after a write.

item.name: E2E — Airport CRUD lifecycle

request.description: Creates the KI — Kiribati country that the airport in this flow belongs to.

exec: // Idempotent setup: remove leftovers from a previous run, airport before country (FK)

request.description: Creates ZZT — Kiribati Test Airport, belonging to KI. Extracts iata and Location header for subsequent steps.
Reads the airport created in step 2.

exec: pm.test('city is Kiribati City', () => pm.expect(body.city).to.eql('Kiribati City'));

request.description: Updates the airport's name. Captures the new name for the next assertion step.

exec: pm.test('name updated', () => pm.expect(body.name).to.eql('Kiribati Test Airport Updated'));

request.description: Confirms the update persisted.
Deletes the airport. Expects 204 No Content.
Confirms the resource no longer exists after deletion.
Deletes the KI country now that the airport referencing it is gone.

item.description: Full create → read → update → verify → delete → verify-gone chain for the Airport resource, including its Country FK dependency. Run steps in order; airportIata is propagated automatically via environment variables.

item.name: E2E — Airline CRUD lifecycle

request.description: Creates the KI — Kiribati country that the airline in this flow belongs to.

exec: // Idempotent setup: remove leftovers from a previous run, airline before country (FK)

request.description: Creates ZTA — Kiribati Airways, belonging to KI. Extracts icao and Location header for subsequent steps.
Reads the airline created in step 2.
Updates the airline's name. Captures the new name for the next assertion step.
Confirms the update persisted.
Deletes the airline. Expects 204 No Content.
Confirms the resource no longer exists after deletion.
Deletes the KI country now that the airline referencing it is gone.

item.description: Full create → read → update → verify → delete → verify-gone chain for the Airline resource, including its Country FK dependency. Run steps in order; airlineIcao is propagated automatically via environment variables.

item.name: E2E — Aircraft CRUD lifecycle

request.description: Creates the KI — Kiribati country that the airline in this flow belongs to.

exec: // Idempotent setup: remove leftovers from a previous run, aircraft before airline before country (FK chain)

request.description: Creates ZTA — Kiribati Airways, belonging to KI, that the aircraft in this flow belongs to.
Creates ZT-001 — a Boeing 787-8, belonging to ZTA. Extracts registration and Location header for subsequent steps.
Reads the aircraft created in step 3.
Updates the aircraft's type code and description. Captures the new description for the next assertion step.
Confirms the update persisted.
Deletes the aircraft. Expects 204 No Content.
Confirms the resource no longer exists after deletion.
Deletes the ZTA airline now that the aircraft referencing it is gone.
Deletes the KI country now that the airline referencing it is gone.

item.description: Full create → read → update → verify → delete → verify-gone chain for the Aircraft resource, including its two-level Country → Airline FK dependency chain. Run steps in order; aircraftRegistration is propagated automatically via environment variables.

description.content:
REST API for managing countries, airports, airlines, routes, aircraft, flights, and flight instances.

Contact Support:
 Name: Aviation API Team
 Email: api@aviation.example
